stdout:
**Content Standards**

Blocking failure: the split child tasks are marked `ready` roots but their descriptions are not self-contained enough to justify starting from a blank workspace, and the DAG does not encode their required context. The content standards say an issue must be understandable “with no other context,” but child leaves such as `3a7e13cf` say only “Drop the planning-config struct and its load validation, and remove every flat configuration block,” without stating that this is only after `validate_scope` and plan-content consumers have migrated. The more serious manifestation is in the DAG section below: the leaf tasks are available without their prerequisite waves.

Minor note: the prior unmarked-criteria issue is addressed. The story and task issues now have `## Success Criteria` with `[hard]` IDs. Story labels are kebab slugs (`story:template-registry`, `story:apply-engine`, etc.), and titles are clean.

Concrete fix for the self-contained task text: add one sentence of background to leaf tasks that depend on prior waves. Example for `3a7e13cf`:

```markdown
This runs only after scoped validation and plan-content resolution read from the template registry, so removing `PlanningConfig` cannot strand any consumer.
```

**Coverage Vs Design**

| Design item | Issue | Matches? |
|---|---:|---|
| W1 — Template registry, `.jit/templates.toml` schema/loader, repo `plan` template, additive accessors | `04559558`, tasks `45e7f203`, `8b15b679` | Mostly |
| W2 — Apply engine, interpolation, validate-before-mutate, edge wiring, `move-upstream-to-role` | `643874f2`, tasks `14137e1a`, `73e5e853` | Mostly |
| W3 — Add `jit apply`, switch scaffold atomically, breakdown consumes pre-created B, remove old commands | `3143ad87`, tasks `9cccfe0c`, `e75805f6` | Mostly |
| W4 — Migrate validation/config consumers, remove `PlanningConfig`, remove flat examples | `96fc8cd8`, tasks `c703a4b3`, `3a7e13cf` | Mostly |
| W5 — Migrate skills, memory, docs, bracket id convention | `adc640e4`, tasks `6e38a577`, `c8aa199e` | Yes |
| W6 — Comprehensive tests, property tests, dogfood | `99a0c161`, tasks `840e155c`, `fc414353` | Partly |
| REQ-09 — Manual reseed existing empty planning-node descriptions, “not an engine deliverable” | `99a0c161` / `fc414353` | No, still mis-scoped |

Blocking failure: prior REQ-09 feedback is still unresolved. The plan’s design decisions say “Backfill: manual (not engine) — reseed `1eb0bdfd`/`2937919f` by hand,” but `99a0c161` is still titled “Tests and dogfood for the graph-template engine,” still carries `satisfies:REQ-09`, and still has `TEST-03: The existing empty planning-node descriptions are reseeded.` The new child `fc414353` also merges unrelated work in its title: “Dogfood apply plan; reseed empty planning nodes.”

Concrete fix:

```bash
jit issue create --type task --title "Reseed existing empty planning-node descriptions" --label epic:graph-templates --label story:template-testing-dogfood --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-09
jit dep add <new-reseed-task> 96fc8cd8
jit issue update 99a0c161 --remove-label satisfies:REQ-09
```

Then remove from `99a0c161`:

```markdown
- [ ] [hard] TEST-03: The existing empty planning-node descriptions are reseeded.
```

And split/retitle `fc414353` to dogfood only:

```markdown
Title: Dogfood apply plan on a real container

## Success Criteria
- [ ] [hard] TSTB-01: Applying the plan template on a real container produces the expected bracket.
```

**Dependency DAG Correctness**

Blocking failure: the added leaf tasks are false roots. `jit query available` shows later-wave leaves available now, including `14137e1a`, `73e5e853`, `9cccfe0c`, `e75805f6`, `c703a4b3`, `3a7e13cf`, `6e38a577`, `c8aa199e`, `840e155c`, and `fc414353`. On a blank workspace, these cannot all start. Examples:

- `14137e1a` needs W1’s template types/loader before `apply_template` can compile.
- `9cccfe0c` needs W2’s apply engine before adding `jit apply` dispatch.
- `3a7e13cf` must not remove `PlanningConfig` before `c703a4b3` migrates remaining consumers.
- `6e38a577` and `c8aa199e` reference removed commands and new behavior that do not exist until W4.
- `840e155c` cannot write comprehensive/property tests for an engine and CLI that do not exist.

Concrete fix:

```bash
jit dep add 8b15b679 45e7f203

jit dep add 14137e1a 8b15b679
jit dep add 73e5e853 14137e1a

jit dep add 9cccfe0c 73e5e853
jit dep add e75805f6 73e5e853

jit dep add c703a4b3 9cccfe0c
jit dep add c703a4b3 e75805f6
jit dep add 3a7e13cf c703a4b3

jit dep add 6e38a577 3a7e13cf
jit dep add c8aa199e 3a7e13cf

jit dep add 840e155c 3a7e13cf
jit dep add fc414353 3a7e13cf
```

Blocking failure: the prior over-constraint is still present at story level. `99a0c161` still depends on `adc640e4`, so all W6 story completion is serialized behind skills/docs migration. The plan says W6 depends on “W1–W4; W5 for skill-level dogfood,” not all tests and manual backfill.

Concrete fix:

```bash
jit dep rm 99a0c161 adc640e4
jit dep add 99a0c161 96fc8cd8
```

If a separate skill-level dogfood task is required, create it and make only that task depend on W5:

```bash
jit issue create --type task --title "Dogfood apply plan through migrated skills" --label epic:graph-templates --label story:template-testing-dogfood --label milestone:v1.0 --label satisfies:REQ-08
jit dep add <new-skill-dogfood-task> adc640e4
```

**Structural Integrity**

The prior flat-structure issue is partially addressed: W1-W6 are now `type:story` issues with `story:*` labels, and each has child tasks. The containment spine is present.

Blocking failure: the structure is still not operationally intact because child tasks are not constrained by the story wave order. The graph now has multi-level labels, but the leaves are dangling from the intended execution order and appear as ready roots. This defeats the layered decomposition the plan requires.

Concrete fix: apply the dependency commands listed in the DAG section so every leaf either starts legitimately from a blank workspace (`45e7f203`) or waits for the concrete prerequisite leaf/story it needs.

**Prior Review Feedback**

Prior run `16f838a5` had four blocking findings. The unmarked criteria and flat story layer were materially addressed. The REQ-09 mis-scope remains unresolved in `99a0c161` and `fc414353`. The W6 over-constraint remains at `99a0c161 -> adc640e4`. The new child-task split introduced a new blocking reachability defect: most child leaves are ready even though their prerequisite waves have not run.

VERDICT: FAIL
